content.problemStatement:
A Github Repository (Remote Repository) is a **project folder on GitHub.** \
A repo is a storage space that keeps your **project files, commits, and branches** all in one place.

**There are two types of Github Repositories :**
- Public → Anyone can see it
- Private → Only you (and invited people) can see it
---

**Step 1 : Log in to your GitHub account** \
Go to https://github.com
 and make sure you're signed in.

**Step 2 : In the upper-right corner of any page, select , then click New repository** 



**Step 3 : Use the Owner dropdown menu to select the Owner of the repository.**


**Step 4 : Type a name for your repository (maximum 100 characters), and an optional description.**


**Step 5 :Choose Visibility**
- Public → Anyone can see your repo
- Private → Only you (and people you invite) can see it
Choose whichever you prefer.


**Step 6 :Initialize the Repository** \
For beginners, check the box **“Add a README file”**. \
This automatically creates your first file and makes the repo easier to understand. \
(You can skip `.gitignore` and `license` for now.)

**Step 7 :Click `Create repository`.**

You’ve just created your first GitHub repository!
